verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is an EGF-family ligand synthesized as a single-pass type I transmembrane proligand (pro-EREG) that is processed by ADAM-family sheddases (notably ADAM17) to release the soluble mature ligand that signals through EGFR/ERBB4. The TM precursor stage is real and supports juxtacrine signaling, but it is transient and the soluble shed form is the dominant biological actor driving downstream signaling. Anti-EREG programs and biology center on the secreted ligand. Per the sheddase rule, this maps to contextual/dual_localization rather than yes/classical_surface_receptor or secreted_only.
